cataloging_subject.label: Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons from the Middle English Filius Matris Cycle
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloging unit is Christine Thérèse Carpenter's selection of seven Middle English sermons associated with the five Sundays in Lent and Palm Sunday, as represented by OTA item 0033 and U-M CME90033. It is distinct from the complete Middle English Filius matris cycle, its late-twelfth-century Latin source cycle, British Library Harley MS 2276, Carpenter's two-volume dissertation, the OTA plain-text file, the U-M encoding, and the repository XML artifact.

catalog_summary.title: Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons from the Middle English Filius Matris Cycle
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English translator
catalog_summary.date: Fifteenth-century translation; Harley witness circa 1450; edited in a 1984 dissertation

titles.value: Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons from the Middle English Filius Matris Cycle

assertion.notes: A concise discovery title supplied for this edition-defined selection; it is not a medieval title.

titles.value: Middle English version of the Matris Filius sermon cycle. Lent and Palm Sunday sermons
notes: The U-M 245-style title continues with a responsibility statement naming Carpenter and Harley MS 2276.
Recorded as U-M's authority-style title; the Latin wording labels the Middle English translation rather than indicating that Latin is an intrinsic language of this selected text.

titles.value: An Edition of the Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons Contained in the Fifteenth-Century Translation of the Late Twelfth-Century Latin 'Filius Matris' Cycle
notes: This is the dissertation title and is retained as an alternate title for the selection; the dissertation itself remains a separate lineage entity.

agents.name: Christine Thérèse Carpenter
notes: Editor and transcriber of the dissertation edition. The U-M header's AUTHOR field is a bibliographic responsibility statement and must not be interpreted as medieval authorship.

scope.description: The fifteenth-century English rendering of the selected Latin Filius matris sermons
The seven-sermon selection and its dissertation edition
The selected sermons in British Library Harley MS 2276, represented in dissertation volume 1, pages 230-359

assertion.notes: The evidence supports only a broad century for the translation; the witness date and U-M's c.1425 stencil must not be transferred silently to composition.

places.label: England
notes: The current British Library catalog identifies England as the manuscript's origin; the translation's exact locality is unknown.
This broad cultural-origin statement does not claim a particular English region or reuse the witness's production place as an exact translation locality.

language_statements.label: Middle English
notes: Latin is the language of the source cycle and of material Carpenter's apparatus reportedly supplied, but U-M says Latin glosses and side notes were omitted from this digital selection; Latin is therefore not asserted as an intrinsic language facet here.

form_statements.description: All seven sermons are continuous prose. The 2,229 numbered line milestones preserve an arbitrary edition-based reference system and do not indicate verse.

scope.description: The First through Fifth Sundays in Lent and Palm Sunday

summaries.value: A seven-sermon Middle English prose selection from the fifteenth-century translation of the Latin Filius matris cycle. It includes an additional penitential sermon and a gospel sermon for the First Sunday in Lent, then sermons for the Second through Fifth Sundays in Lent and Palm Sunday. The sermons combine exposition of liturgical Gospel readings with instruction on faith, humility, penance, fasting, prayer, almsgiving, obedience, patience, and other Christian virtues.

The selected text corresponds broadly to Harley MS 2276, folios 52v-70v, and to Carpenter's dissertation, volume 1, pages 230-359. The current BL catalog describes the larger witness as a sixty-sermon Middle English collection on folios 1r-146v, followed by an index on folios 146v-148v.

extent.description: Seven DIV1 sermon units containing 116 prose paragraphs. The complete XML has 120 P elements when three header paragraphs and the one-paragraph argument are included. Computational inspection also found 2,229 numbered line milestones, 37 folio milestones, 2,077 LB elements, and 173 Q elements; the preserved lineation is editorial/reference layout, not verse.

content_parts.label: [Þis is] a Sermoun on þe First Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: An additional First-Sunday-in-Lent sermon on faith, hope, charity, confession, penance, vigils, fasting, prayer, and almsgiving.

content_parts.label: Þe first Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: A First-Sunday-in-Lent gospel sermon centered on Christ's example, humility, fasting, and victory over temptation.

locators.value: begins on f. 55r; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 55v-f. 57v

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone; it is not independently marked at this DIV1 start.

content_parts.label: Þe seconde Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: A sermon on the Canaanite woman, faith, penance, humility, and perseverance in virtue.

locators.value: begins on f. 57v; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 58r-f. 60r

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone.

content_parts.label: Þe þrid Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: A gospel exposition on Christ's exorcism, spiritual interpretation, divine teaching, and resistance to vice.

locators.value: begins on f. 60r; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 60v-f. 63r

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone.

content_parts.label: Þe fourþe Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: An exposition of the feeding of the multitude and its spiritual meaning, including Christ as the bread of life.

locators.value: begins on f. 63r; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 63v-f. 66r

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone.

content_parts.label: Þe fifþe Sunday of Lentoun
content_parts.summary: A gospel sermon on Christ's controversy with his opponents, emphasizing patience, truth, and spiritual understanding.

locators.value: begins on f. 66r; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 66v-f. 68v

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone.

content_parts.label: [Here bigynneþ be Gospel on] Palm Sunday
content_parts.summary: An exposition of Christ's entry into Jerusalem, with instruction on obedience, humility, and virtuous conduct.

locators.value: begins on f. 68v; internal U-M folio milestones run f. 69r-f. 70v

notes: The opening folio boundary is inferred from the preceding sermon's final milestone.

related_works.title: Middle English Filius matris sermon cycle
notes: The selected seven sermons are part of a larger English cycle; Harley MS 2276 contains sixty sermons across folios 1r-146v.

related_works.title: Collectio Filius matris
notes: A late-twelfth- or early-thirteenth-century Latin sermon collection traditionally attributed to William de Montibus. The traditional attribution is not transferred to authorship of the English translation.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository, CME90033.xml, HEADER, ARGUMENT, and opening metadata at pinned submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Supplies the selected title and identifiers, Carpenter responsibility, dissertation source statement, OTA ancestry, Middle English language, editorial history, dissertation page locus, and Harley manuscript attribution.
evidence.locator: HEADER; EEBO/IDG; EEBO/TEXT/HEAD, OPENER, and ARGUMENT

evidence.citation: General Corpus inspection of the approved CME90033 XML source, 2026-07-14.
evidence.summary: Confirmed seven DIV1 sermon units, their headings and sequence, 116 sermon P elements (120 P elements in the complete XML), 2,229 numbered line milestones, 37 folio milestones, 2,077 LB elements, 173 Q elements, prose form, and the broad ff. 52v-70v witness locus.

evidence.citation: General Corpus publication-set source override for CME90033.
evidence.summary: Confirms that the recent_changes/additions_2023 XML, rather than the sparse-header CME_from_OTA duplicate, is the source bound to the published CME90033 PDF.

evidence.citation: British Library, 'Middle English sermon collection,' Harley MS 2276, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, record 040-002048107.
evidence.summary: Describes the circa-1450 English parchment witness, its sixty-sermon Filius matris translation at ff. 1r-146v, index at ff. 146v-148v, physical extent, script, and English origin.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, 'British Library. Manuscript. Harley 2276,' OTA item 0033, full record.
evidence.summary: Confirms the public OTA item, contributor Carpenter, Middle English language, broad 1400-1499 date, public status, CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 statement, and downloadable harley2276-0033.txt file; its six-occasion abstract is less granular than the seven sermon blocks in the file.

evidence.citation: Alastair Bennett, 'A Middle English Sermon Based on Gregory the Great's Homily for the Second Sunday in Advent,' Mediaeval Studies 83 (2021): 27-57, especially 29 n. 7.
evidence.summary: States that the late-twelfth-century Latin Filius matris cycle was translated into Middle English in the fifteenth century and gives the full Carpenter dissertation citation as a partial edition from Bedford College, University of London, 1984.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Handlist XXIV: Cambridge University Library MS Kk.6.2, Cambridge Core summary.
evidence.summary: Describes a full English translation of the late-twelfth- or early-thirteenth-century Latin Collectio Filius matris, traditionally attributed to William de Montibus, and identifies Harley 2276 among the English witnesses.

open_questions.question: Can the Middle English translation's date, locality, translator, or relationship among the surviving English witnesses be established more precisely?
next_steps: Consult Carpenter's complete dissertation, Helen Spencer's 1977 study, and current scholarship on the complete English Filius matris tradition.
Keep witness production data separate from composition data.

open_questions.question: What are the exact manuscript start folios and dissertation page ranges for each of the seven sermons?
next_steps: Inspect a Harley MS 2276 facsimile and Carpenter's dissertation.
Use the U-M-added milestones only for the broad ff. 52v-70v span until every DIV1 opening can be verified independently.

open_questions.question: How do the OTA record's 1980 issue date, the U-M header's 1983 OTA date, and the 1984 dissertation relate bibliographically?
next_steps: Request or locate OTA deposit history and inspect Carpenter's dissertation preliminaries.
Preserve the three dated assertions at their own layers in the meantime.

notes: The OTA catalog abstract says the file contains one sermon for each of five Lent Sundays plus Palm Sunday, but the downloadable file and U-M XML contain seven sermon blocks because the First Sunday in Lent has two separate sermons; the inspected text structure governs the item count.

The repository publication override binds CME90033.pdf to the full-header recent_changes/additions_2023 source. The alternate CME_from_OTA XML has a materially different header, although the canonicalized EEBO bodies are byte-identical; publication and upstream identifiers are therefore retained as aliases without conflating artifact layers.

Catalog and rights assertions belong to their named layers and do not transfer automatically among the medieval work, witness, dissertation, OTA text, U-M encoding, repository artifact, or any future manuscript photographs.
